title: Transformation of the chlorarachniophyte Amorphochlora amoebiformis by electroporation

The Plant Journal. 64: 402-410.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"615F0545341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":563,"is_image":true,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"3nefk\",\"text\":\"\\u003epLaRbcS182+GFP\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":[]},{\"key\":\"6o5f7\",\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":[]}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"pLaRbcS182+GFP.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/bakkan.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bakkan.png","width":688}}}}
steps: Propagate plasmid DNA (pLaRGfp or its derivatives, such as pLaR182+GFP) in the Escherichia coli strain DH5α. Internal plastid-targeting signal found in a RubisCO small subunit protein of a chlorarachniophyte alga. The Plant Journal. Transient transformation of a chlorarachniophyte alga, Lotharella amoebiformis (chlorarachniophyceae), with uidA and egfp reporter genes. Journal of Phycology.
Purify plasmid DNA from 200 mL culture of E. coli by a Qiagen Plasmid Maxi Kit (Qiagen).
Adjust plasmid DNA concentration to 3-5 µg/µL with distilled water.

Culture Amorphochlora amoebiformis (CCMP2058) cells in 500 mL Erlenmeyer flasks containing 200 mL ESM medium at 20°C under white illumination (50-80 μmol photons·m −2 ·s −1 ) on a 14:10 hours light:dark cycle for a week.
After decanting medium, resuspend cells adhered to the bottom of flasks by gentile pipetting with 2 to 3 mL ESM medium (use a glass pipette). Approximately 4×10 7 cells can be obtained from a flask.
Harvest a total of 5×10 6 cells by centrifugation at 2,000 g for 5 sec (use a mini centrifuge).
Resuspend cell pellet in 100 µL of Gene Pulser electroporation buffer (Bio-Rad) with 10 µL of plasmid DNA at the room temperature.
Transfer cell solution into electroporation cuvette with 0.2 cm gap (Bio-Rad).
Electroporate cells with a 25 ms square wave pulse at 120 V using Gene Pulser Xcell Electroporation System.
Add 0.9 mL fresh ESM medium to cuvette immediately after electroporation.
Transfer cells to glass bottom well plate/petri dish, and add an appropriate volume of ESM medium.
Incubate cells for 24 hours before observation of GFP fluorescence.
creator: Yoshihisa Hirakawa
